todos.text: [PARKED] DISCUSS: Pretend you are the developer of a competing AI dev tool (e.g. get-shit-done, aider, cursor, or similar) evaluating FORGE for the first time. Give an honest external assessment: (1) STRENGTHS — what would genuinely impress you compared to your own tool? Think: gate system, pipeline structure, specialised agents, signal protocol, wave execution, skills system, the fact that FORGE develops itself; (2) WEAKNESSES — what would make you uncomfortable or unconvinced? Think: token cost, pipeline complexity, orchestrator reliability, onboarding friction for new users, dependency on Claude CLI, no test automation, single-user focus; (3) MISSING — what features does your tool have that FORGE clearly lacks? (4) OVER-ENGINEERED — what would you call unnecessarily complex for the value it delivers? (5) VERDICT — would you be worried about FORGE as a competitor, and why or why not? Be harsh. The goal is to find real gaps and blind spots before real users do.

todos.text: FEATURE: Restart pipeline from a specific mid-pipeline stage (e.g. "coder finished, reviewers crashed — restart from reviewers"). Plumbing partially exists: `stages` field tracks per-stage status, `forge_advance_stage` handles inter-phase transitions (plan→implement), `/forge:resume` restores steering context. What's missing: skills don't read completed sub-steps to skip ahead — re-invoking a skill always starts the agent sequence from the beginning. Needs: (1) skills check stages/artifacts to detect already-completed sub-steps and skip them, (2) UX for specifying restart point (e.g. `/forge:resume r-xxx --from reviewers`).

todos.text: FEATURE: Evaluation harness — replay past pipeline runs to measure agent quality. Capture reproducible inputs (plan docs, handoffs, source snapshots from run worktrees) and compare agent outputs against expected results. Example evaluations: did reviewer-safety catch a known vulnerability? Did the planner scope a feature correctly? Did the coder's handoff match the plan? Requires: (1) snapshot mechanism to freeze run inputs at each stage, (2) pass/fail criteria per agent type, (3) a runner script that feeds snapshots to agents and scores outputs. Only valuable once enough historical runs exist to replay. Future vision — no existing infrastructure.

FEATURE: Data flow preview before pipeline runs. Structured pre-flight step that shows which files will be touched and what changes in each.

**Approach:** Node.js script (like coder-scout.mjs) that parses docs/PLAN.md task lines, extracts backtick-quoted file paths, and outputs a JSON manifest: files, actions (create/modify), which tasks touch each file, risk surface tags. **Deferred until coder-writes-source (slices 1-3) lands** — pipeline shape is still changing. Design against the stable pipeline, not the current one.

**Priority:** low — manual conversation format works for now (enforced by feedback memory). Automate once pipeline stabilizes.

todos.text: ANTI-SPECULATION HARDENING — Stage 4 hook-trigger broken on Windows. Stages 1-3 SHIPPED. Stage 4 INFRASTRUCTURE WORKS end-to-end when invoked manually: temp jsonl at os.tmpdir()/claude-audit-<sessionId>.jsonl is written by hooks/ctx-post-tool.js:51 (282 files in temp confirm flow), and scripts/audit-tool-calls.mjs reads + writes findings to docs/audit-log.jsonl. PROVEN: ran `node scripts/audit-tool-calls.mjs --root . --session <newest>` manually this session, produced a real blind-write finding for the active implement run. The chain that's broken is the SubagentStop hook trigger. hooks/audit-trigger.js:43-46 spawns audit-tool-calls.mjs with `{ detached: true, stdio: 'ignore' }` and immediately exits. On Windows, detached+unref does not reliably keep the child alive past the parent hook's termination — the spawned node process gets killed before it can write. Glob confirms zero audit-log.jsonl files anywhere on disk despite hundreds of subagent stops over recent sessions. Fix directions: (a) replace detached spawn with synchronous execFileSync (audit takes <100ms; non-blocking is overkill), (b) write a marker file the hook waits for before exiting, or (c) move audit invocation to ctx-stop.js (session end) so the worker doesn't race with the audit. Once the trigger reliably writes audit-log.jsonl, surface aggregated metrics in mcp/lib/dashboard-state.js SPECS tab. Close conditions: docs/audit-log.jsonl populated by hook-triggered runs (not just manual invocation) AND visible in observer SPECS tab.
todos.title: ANTI-SPECULATION HARDENING
todos.summary: Stages 1-3 SHIPPED.

todos.text: FIX: Duplicate agent tracking — run-active.json agents[] vs run.json agents[]. subagent-start.js writes agent entries to run-active.json (ephemeral session pointer), while run.json also has an agents[] array populated by forge_update_run from skills. These two representations drift — run-active.json is the live tracker but run.json is the durable record. Unify: either have subagent-start/stop write directly to the run registry (via forge_update_run), or have a reconciliation step at run completion.

todos.text: BUG: Discarding a run doesn't kill the worker process. forge_update_run(status: "discarded") only updates the registry — the worker keeps running and can overwrite the status back to gate-pending when it reaches a gate. Fix: forge_update_run (or a new forge_discard_run tool) should write the poison pill file (.pipeline/worker-kill/<runId>) when transitioning to "discarded". The worker already watches for this file via watchFile (forge-worker.mjs:62-70) and exits gracefully on detection.

todos.summary: forge_update_run(status: "discarded") only updates the registry — the worker keeps running and can overwrite the status back to gate-pending when it reaches a gate.

todos.text: Merge commit gate approve+commit into single step. Currently apply pipeline requires 3 user actions: "apply" → "approve" → "commit". Change: (1) Remove separate approve step for commit gates — the commit gate is the only gate after apply. (2) Accept both "approve" and "commit" as valid unlock keywords for commit gates in workflow-guard.js hasValidGateApprovalToken. (3) When the conductor sees an approved commit gate, immediately chain to commit+merge (no second prompt). Result: "apply" → wait for worker → "commit" → done. Cuts approve fatigue and fixes the "commit" keyword not being recognized as authorization.
todos.title: Merge commit gate approve+commit

todos.text: forge_check_gate returns stale main-root gate file when current gate is in a worktree. forge_set_gate syncs worktree→main on write, but the sync doesn't always happen (e.g. worker writes gate directly). todos.text: Enforce single worker session from plan through implement through commit. Currently the conductor creates a new run+worker for /forge:implement after gate1 approval, losing all context. The worker already handles gate1 resume via streamInput (forge-worker.mjs lines 196-236) — the conductor must NOT create a new run. Instead: gate1 approval resumes the existing worker, which continues to the implement stage in the same session. Matches the single-runId lifecycle design in docs/brainstorms/pipeline-redesign.md. Fix: (1) conductor inline gate approval must not suggest "/forge:implement" after gate1 — the worker auto-resumes. (2) CLAUDE.md gate1 instructions must say "worker resumes automatically" not "run /forge:implement". (3) /forge:implement skill should check for an existing plan run and advance it via forge_advance_stage instead of creating a new run.

todos.text: Per-run agent audit trail. Add .pipeline/runs/<runId>/agent-audit.json that tracks the full agent pipeline from classification to execution. Schema: { classifier: { riskLevel, reviewers, advisories }, conductor: { approvedAgents, approvedAt }, worker: { dispatched: [{ agent, at }] } }. Conductor writes classifier + conductor sections at run creation (skill Step 1). subagent-start.js hook appends worker.dispatched entries as agents are spawned. Enables: (1) verify conductor-locked pipeline is enforced (approved vs dispatched match), (2) identify never-used agents for cleanup, (3) audit data for prompt quality improvements.

todos.text: BUG: Observer card disappears before commit+merge. Gate2 approval sets run status to "completed" prematurely — card moves from active to completed section before the conductor finishes commit+merge. The SKILL.md fix (bdf1b59) is necessary but insufficient: stale SKILL context in the session can override it. Root fix: harden forge_update_run MCP handler to reject gate-pending→completed transitions without an approval token, even when the model passes status explicitly. The guard at mcp/server.js:1936-1946 already exists but only fires when cleanPatch.status is set — needs to also block implicit completions or add a secondary check. DESIGN PROPOSAL (2026-05-03): Replace single status:"completed" with lifecycle milestones — gate1ApprovedAt, gate2ApprovedAt, mergedAt. Run is only "completed" when mergedAt is populated. Observer shows gate-pending runs with approved gateState as "approved — awaiting commit" instead of removing them. This gives full visibility into what the session has been through. NEEDS DISCUSSION before implementation — scope and migration strategy TBD.

todos.summary: Gate2 approval sets run status to "completed" prematurely — card moves from active to completed section before the conductor finishes commit+merge.

todos.text: Approval token location mismatch: approval-token.js hook writes action-approved.json to cwd's .pipeline/ (worktree when cwd is a worktree), but MCP server's hasGateApprovalToken() always reads from resolveProjectDir() (main root). When a commit gate fires in a worktree session, the token is never found. Workaround: manually copy token to main root. Fix: hook should write to both locations, or MCP should check both.

On machines without Windows Terminal, the observer never launches. Fix: forge.cmd should open observer in a separate cmd window via `start cmd /k` when wt.exe is missing. observer-autosplit.js should do the same via spawn. User gets two windows instead of split panes, but still has both surfaces.

todos.text: Critic agent ignores focus-area prompt — when invoked with "agent and skill architecture review" focus, produced 0 architectural findings and 10 code-level hook/MCP bugs. The agent's Step 2 scanning heuristics (largest files, entry points) dominate over any focus-area context in the dispatch prompt. Fix options: (a) add a "focus area" parameter to the critic agent that constrains which files/lenses it prioritizes, (b) create a separate architecture-critic agent that reviews agent definitions, skill contracts, and pipeline design rather than source code, (c) add a Step 1.5 that reads the dispatch prompt for focus keywords and filters the file scan accordingly.

Fix: reset the timeout each time a gate is detected (line 210), so the 30-min window restarts per gate rather than covering the entire worker lifetime.

The Claude SDK stream is already exhausted when the implement session finishes, so streamInput() fails. The gate detection at line 208 only runs inside the for-await loop — if the stream ends before gate2 is approved, the resume injection has no transport. Fix: after gate2 approval, create a fresh query() stream instead of injecting into the dead one.

todos.text: Per-run pipeline state — fix singleton run-active.json + narrow collision guard. Concurrent multi-worktree pipelines are blocked today by the collision guard (mcp/server.js:1710-1717, 2521-2528) which serializes ALL forge_create_run + forge_advance_stage calls. The guard exists because run-active.json is a singleton (server.js:1657) consumed by hooks that have no per-run identity: subagent-stop.js:137-275 (wholesale agent-array overwrite — same as bug TODO 463821c7), ctx-stop.js:38,66 (completeness check), workflow-guard.js:79 (apply-gate enforcement), forge-status.js:172, dashboard-state.js:26. Fix: (1) replace run-active.json singleton with per-run markers (e.g. .pipeline/runs/<runId>/active.json or worktree-local), (2) workers identify their run via env var FORGE_RUN_ID or by resolving worktree path, (3) hooks read the run-specific marker, (4) THEN narrow collision guard to "same working directory only" so different worktrees + read-only runs (ideate, research) can run concurrently. Also fix orphan-run bug: forge_create_run writes run + sets status=running at server.js:1648-1697 BEFORE the collision check at 1710, so blocked dispatches leave zombie runs (saw r-ec1537c8 today). Closes the existing agent-outcome-null bug as a side effect. Realizes the worktree-conductor multi-session vision.

todos.text: Stuck-loop hard-stop — token burn from infinite agent retries is unprotected at the SubagentStart layer. The dead deny-envelope code at hooks/subagent-start.js:124-139 has been REMOVED (replaced with a loud stderr warning so stuck loops are at least visible in logs). What remains: a REAL stop mechanism for runaway agent dispatch loops. SubagentStart cannot block — needs upstream intervention. Options: (a) PreToolUse hook on the Agent tool that reads run-active.json agents array and denies a 3rd+ dispatch of the same agent_type (PreToolUse CAN deny); (b) the worker harness watches the agents array on stream messages and aborts via poison-pill when a loop is detected; (c) the SDK's maxTurns or stop conditions configured per-run. Until one of these lands, repeated dispatches will burn tokens silently except for the stderr warning.

Also worth investigating: was the rerun on r-fcedb742 due to the existing 0e05f1ab bug (first instance's completedAt was clobbered) — if so, fixing 0e05f1ab eliminates the rerun, halving the token spend on this agent regardless.

todos.text: Reset worker timer after each phase commit — preserves safety-valve semantic with phased execution. mcp/forge-worker.mjs:112 has WORKER_TIMEOUT_MS (just raised from 30min to 60min as quick fix). The timer's resetWorkerTimer() function (mcp/forge-worker.mjs:125) currently fires only on gate transitions (gate-pending entry + approval), not on per-phase commits. Result: a 4-phase implement burns one shared budget instead of getting fresh time per phase. Even with 60min, a 6+ phase plan can hit the cap mid-stream. Fix: in skills/implement/SKILL.md per-phase loop (Step 2c-ish), after each phase's worktree commit succeeds, signal the worker to call resetWorkerTimer(). Mechanism options: (a) write a sentinel file to .pipeline/worker-reset/<runId> that the worker watches, (b) extend an existing MCP tool (forge_update_run with phases? new forge_reset_worker_timer?), (c) use the existing watchFile pattern from poison-pill at mcp/forge-worker.mjs:139. Discovered: r-fcedb742 hit the original 30-min cap with Phase 1 (9min) + Phase 2 (10min) + Phase 3 partial (~13min) = ~32min, killing the worker mid-Phase-3 reviewers and forcing manual Phase 3 salvage.

todos.text: Stop committing mcp/node_modules — gitignore it and rely on npm ci. ESCALATED to high priority after r-9192b81f spawn failure: the merge of r-d5b1ccd9 left 52 tracked node_modules files deleted from main's working tree (including @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk itself, blocking any worker spawn). Required manual `git restore mcp/node_modules` to recover. Current state (.gitignore:1-5): mcp/node_modules is committed as an offline fallback so the MCP server boots before npm ci runs. Cost: 272 MB committed in main, 272 MB duplicated per worktree, AND occasional silent deletion via merge cascades when the per-worktree copy gets out of sync with main during merge resolution. Plan: (1) add `mcp/node_modules/` to .gitignore (remove the `!mcp/node_modules/` exception), (2) `git rm -r --cached mcp/node_modules`, (3) verify hooks/mcp-deps-install.js runs npm ci on SessionStart and the MCP server starts cleanly on first boot, (4) document the new contract (npm required for first run) in docs/gotchas/GENERAL.md and the README. Net effect: ~272 MB shrink per worktree + same on main, faster git clones, smaller PRs, ELIMINATES the merge-cascade-deletion class of incidents that just bit us. Risk: offline first-boot breaks (mitigation: most users have network when installing a Claude Code plugin).

todos.text: Plan-stage reviewers fail silently when stale output files exist — directory-hygiene bug. Symptom: across r-ad7b145e and r-d5b1ccd9, exactly 2 of 4 reviewers (safety + logic) showed completedAt: null with no fresh output file written, while the other 2 (boundary + performance) succeeded. Root cause traced this turn: ROOT CAUSE — Claude Code's Write tool blocks writing to a file that hasn't been Read in the current session ("File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it"). The previous run's reviewer-*.md files persist in docs/context/reviewer-output/ and any reviewer trying to write to them in the next run gets blocked. Evidence: r-d5b1ccd9 reviewer-safety log shows is_error:true with that exact message; reviewer-logic.md mtime stayed at 19:12:14 (prev run) despite reviewer-logic agent prompt at agents/reviewer-logic.md:65,141 mandating Write. The implement skill already handles this via skills/implement/SKILL.md:155 ("clear reviewer-output between phases") but skills/plan/SKILL.md never clears the directory before dispatching plan-stage reviewers. Fix: add a step to skills/plan/SKILL.md (and skills/refactor + skills/debug if they have similar reviewer dispatch) that deletes docs/context/reviewer-output/*.md before invoking reviewers. Per-run isolation in worktrees mitigates this for implement-stage but plan stage runs in main root and shares the directory across runs. Until fixed: every plan run sees flaky reviewer behaviour with no clear signal that anything is wrong (completedAt is null, that's it).

todos.text: Add deletion-detection sanity check to bin/forge-worktree.js merge — defensive belt-and-suspenders for accidental tracked-file deletion during merge cascade. Symptom (this session): after `bin/forge-worktree.js merge` failed twice and was completed manually, 52 tracked mcp/node_modules files were deleted from main's working tree (still in HEAD/index, missing on disk) — including @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk which blocked the next worker spawn. Required manual `git restore mcp/node_modules` to recover. Fix: in bin/forge-worktree.js, after `git worktree remove --force` at line 245 (success path) and 271 (error path) — before exiting — run `git status --short`, parse for entries starting with ` D ` (working-tree deletion of a tracked file), and if any are found: (a) log a loud warning with the list, (b) auto-run `git restore <path>` for each (since the deletion is unintentional from the caller's perspective), (c) surface the recovery in the JSON output (`{ ok: true, restoredFiles: [...] }`). ~15 lines of node script in bin/forge-worktree.js. Distinct from TODO 0233f9c8 (gitignore mcp/node_modules) — that fix eliminates the biggest target but this guard catches the broader class of accidental-deletion-during-merge bugs that could affect any tracked file.
todos.title: Add deletion-detection sanity check
todos.summary: Symptom (this session): after `bin/forge-worktree.js merge` failed twice and was completed manually, 52 tracked mcp/node_modules files were deleted from main's working tree (still in HEAD/index, missing on disk) — including @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk which blocked the next worker spawn.

todos.text: Worker logs land in wrong path for worktree-backed runs — observer always reports stale. Symptom (r-c69ce245): worker spawned with useWorktree=true, cwd=.worktrees/r-c69ce245/. The worker log was written to .worktrees/r-c69ce245/.pipeline/worker-logs/r-c69ce245.log (270 KB, fresh) but the run.json's logFile field and the observer's read path both point to main's .pipeline/worker-logs/r-c69ce245.log (empty). Result: observer staleness check sees an empty log frozen at spawn time and flags every worktree-backed run as dead within seconds. Verified via Glob this turn — worktree log exists with current mtime; main log empty. Root cause: mcp/forge-worker.mjs writes logs at join(workDir, '.pipeline', 'worker-logs', runId + '.log') where workDir = process.cwd() = the worktree path when useWorktree=true. Two fix directions: (a) worker always logs to MAIN's .pipeline/worker-logs/ regardless of worktree (resolve main project dir from worker, write there) — preserves single canonical log location, simpler observer logic; (b) observer reads worktree log when run.worktreePath is set — keeps logs co-located with their run but adds path resolution complexity to every consumer. Recommend (a) since logs are operational state, not run artifacts. Affects every worktree-backed run end-to-end. Blocks the multi-session vision from being usefully observable. Discovered while validating per-run pipeline state work.
todos.title: Worker logs land in wrong path for
todos.summary: Symptom (r-c69ce245): worker spawned with useWorktree=true, cwd=.worktrees/r-c69ce245/.

todos.text:
Zombie worker prevention — proven reliability blocker (yesterday's session held .pipeline/run-active.json locked, forced MCP server kill).
(a) mcp/forge-worker.mjs adds periodic readRunData() poll — if run.status is terminal (completed/failed/discarded/merged), exit gracefully.
(b) forge_create_run / forge_advance_stage scan .pipeline/worker-pids/ on entry; for any PID whose run is in terminal status, kill the PID before proceeding.
Together these prevent zombie workers from accumulating and locking shared state.
todos.title: Zombie worker prevention
todos.summary: (a) mcp/forge-worker.mjs adds periodic readRunData() poll — if run.status is terminal (completed/failed/discarded/merged), exit gracefully.

todos.text: BUG: completeness-checker not dispatched during implement stage despite being in stages.implement.agents. Observed in r-e31a1621 (cited from forge_get_run agents trail 2026-05-06): stages.implement.agents = ["coder-scout","coder","completeness-checker"] but no forge:completeness-checker entry in agents array — only coder + reviewer-safety + reviewer-boundary ran. The implement skill or worker is skipping completeness-checker even when explicitly listed. This is the recurring miss the user has flagged. Repro: dispatch any /forge:implement with completeness-checker in stages.implement.agents and observe the agents array post-handoff. Likely root cause: implement skill's agent-dispatch loop hardcodes coder + reviewers and ignores other entries in stages.implement.agents. Fix path: skills/implement/SKILL.md must dispatch every agent listed in stages.implement.agents, not a hardcoded subset.

Observed in r-e31a1621 (cited from forge_get_run agents trail 2026-05-06): plan stage dispatched all 4 reviewers (safety, boundary, logic, performance — all REVISE). Post-coder dispatch only ran safety + boundary (both APPROVED). reviewer-logic raised AC-4 readRunData ambiguity and reviewer-performance raised resolveMainProjectRoot caching — both were addressed in code organically, but the post-handoff classifier did not surface them for verification. The post-handoff dispatcher (scripts/lean-risk-classify.mjs / reviewer-dispatch.mjs) should consider PLAN.md REVISE history when a previous stage explicitly flagged logic/performance concerns. Fix path: when a feature has prior plan-stage REVISE verdicts from reviewer-logic or reviewer-performance, the post-handoff dispatch should include those reviewers regardless of pattern match.

todos.text: BUG: forge:documenter agent silently reports completion without writing files. Observed twice in r-0e6834e6 (cited from this session's manual documenter dispatches 2026-05-06). First dispatch: 30K tokens, 10 tool uses, agent's last message was "Now I need to determine if a solution doc is needed... Let me check if docs/solutions/ directory exists" (mid-investigation). Second dispatch with explicit pre-located inputs and tighter scope: 28K tokens, 4 tool uses, returned "docs: changelog + solution" but `git status --short` showed zero new or modified files post-dispatch. Documenter frontmatter `tools:` includes Write/Edit so capability isn't the constraint. Possible causes to investigate: (a) documenter is producing terse "plan" output instead of executing the plan, (b) documenter is using an in-memory model session that exits before flushing Write calls, (c) documenter's haiku model is skipping the write step under maxTurns:10. Manually wrote CHANGELOG.md and docs/solutions/preToolUse-counter-file-pattern.md as fallback for r-0e6834e6.

todos.summary: Observed twice in r-0e6834e6 (cited from this session's manual documenter dispatches 2026-05-06).

todos.text: Approval token UX: extend TTL from 5 min to 30 min, auto-bundle commit with gate-approve so a single 'approve' for a commit gate doesn't require a separate 'commit' keyword later. Surface: hooks/approval-token.js (TOKEN_TTL_MS at line 21, ACTION_KEYWORDS at lines 24-28, auto-include logic at lines 119-129). AC1: TOKEN_TTL_MS = 1_800_000 (30 min). AC2: when ACTIONS.includes('gate-approve'), unconditionally include 'commit' too (currently only does so when a commit gate file exists — friction observed when user types 'approve' for commit gate but token expires before next git commit). AC3: keyword set extended to recognize 'go' and 'yes' as gate-approve triggers when context is unambiguous (gate-pending run exists). Justification: this session required 4-5 retypes due to expiration and narrow keyword set.
todos.title: Approval token UX
todos.summary: Surface: hooks/approval-token.js (TOKEN_TTL_MS at line 21, ACTION_KEYWORDS at lines 24-28, auto-include logic at lines 119-129).

todos.text: /forge:approve gate1 auto-commits uncommitted PLAN.md edits before advance_stage. Pattern: user edits main's docs/PLAN.md to address REVISE feedback, conductor approves gate1, advance_stage spawns worker which branches off main HEAD (without my edits), merge conflict at end. Observed 3 times this session (r-e31a1621, r-0e6834e6, r-48efcaef — only the last avoided it because user reminded me). Surface: skills/approve/SKILL.md Step 3 gate1 branch. AC1: between forge_set_gate(gate1, approved) and forge_advance_stage, run `git diff --name-only docs/PLAN.md` and `git status --short docs/PLAN.md`; if PLAN.md has unstaged or staged-but-uncommitted changes, commit them with message 'plan(forge): incorporate REVISE feedback for <feature>' BEFORE advance_stage. AC2: if no changes, skip silently. AC3: never amend an existing commit; always new commit. AC4: if commit fails (hook failure, permission), log warning and proceed with advance_stage anyway (fail-open — user can resolve manually).
todos.title: /forge:approve gate1 auto-commits
todos.summary: Pattern: user edits main's docs/PLAN.md to address REVISE feedback, conductor approves gate1, advance_stage spawns worker which branches off main HEAD (without my edits), merge conflict at end.

todos.text: /forge:apply Step 2a worktree resolution: filter by stage progression, not pipelineType. Currently Step 2a says 'Use forge_list_runs filtered by pipelineType values implement/refactor/debug to find all completed runs for this feature'. Plan-pipeline runs that progressed through implement stage have pipelineType='plan' (unchanged from create_run), so apply doesn't find their worktrees. Observed for r-0e6834e6 (pipelineType=plan, worktreePath populated, implement stage completed) — required manual commit+merge bypass. Surface: skills/apply/SKILL.md Step 2a. AC1: change filter to (run.worktreePath != null) && (run.stages.implement.status === 'completed' OR run.stages.debug.status === 'completed' OR run.stages.refactor.status === 'completed'). AC2: pipelineType filter is removed. AC3: if multiple matches, take the most recent by createdAt. AC4: if no match, skill operates in main project root as today.

todos.summary: Currently Step 2a says 'Use forge_list_runs filtered by pipelineType values implement/refactor/debug to find all completed runs for this feature'.

todos.text: Worker context-window logging: emit structured progress line every N messages so silent worker death is observable post-mortem. Pattern: r-0e6834e6 worker died after coder completed (~233s) and before reviewer dispatch — log just stopped, no exit reason, no error. Surface: mcp/forge-worker.mjs main message loop. AC1: every 25 messages (or every 60s, whichever first), worker writes a single JSON line to stderr: {type:'forge-worker-progress', runId, messagesSeen, agentsCompleted, lastAgentType, contextEstimate, timestamp}. AC2: contextEstimate is best-effort — count cache_creation_input_tokens + input_tokens from message usage fields, divided by model context limit (claude-haiku-4-5: 200k). AC3: line is one-shot per dispatch boundary, not per-message — debounced. AC4: useful for /forge:resume to know whether the worker hit context wall vs crashed. Cross-references TODO 8d1daaef (completeness-checker not dispatched — same silent-death class).
todos.title: Worker context-window logging
todos.summary: Pattern: r-0e6834e6 worker died after coder completed (~233s) and before reviewer dispatch — log just stopped, no exit reason, no error.

todos.text: bash-guard: read-vs-write distinction so read-only file inspections through node -e are not blocked. Pattern: bash-guard substring-matches paths like .pipeline/gate-pending.json and blocks any node -e command containing the path, even pure readFileSync. Forces tool-switch to Read which is correct in many cases but breaks programmatic JSON reads where we need to parse and project. Surface: hooks/bash-guard.js. AC1: distinguish write operations (writeFileSync, writeFile, unlink, rename, rm, rmdir, mkdir, createWriteStream, appendFile) from read operations (readFileSync, readFile, existsSync, statSync, readdirSync). AC2: read-only commands referencing protected .pipeline/ paths are allowed. AC3: write/delete to protected paths still blocked. AC4: heuristic is conservative — if a command contains BOTH read and write fs calls referencing protected paths, block. Justification: forces tool-switching multiple times this session for inspect-only operations.
todos.title: bash-guard
todos.summary: Pattern: bash-guard substring-matches paths like .pipeline/gate-pending.json and blocks any node -e command containing the path, even pure readFileSync.

todos.text: /forge:cleanup skill: sweep merged-but-not-cleaned worktrees and their branches in one user-authorized step. Pattern: after /forge:approve commit-gate merges a worktree branch, the worktree dir + branch remain because git worktree remove refuses on dirty files (runtime artifacts in worktree's .pipeline/) and bash-guard blocks --force. Observed: .worktrees/r-e31a1621 and .worktrees/r-0e6834e6 both still on disk at end of this session. Surface: NEW skill skills/cleanup/SKILL.md, plus a bash-guard exception path scoped to this skill. AC1: skill scans .worktrees/ directory; for each entry, looks up the corresponding run via runId in name; if run.status is in ['completed','failed','discarded','merged'], the entry is a cleanup candidate. AC2: for each candidate, runs `git worktree remove --force <path>` followed by `git branch -d <branchName>` (force is permitted because the run is terminal). AC3: bash-guard.js gets a context check (e.g. CLAUDE_PLUGIN_RUNNING_SKILL=cleanup env var) that allows --force on git worktree remove ONLY when set. AC4: skill never deletes a worktree whose run.status is non-terminal. AC5: dry-run mode shows what would be deleted without acting.
todos.title: /forge:cleanup skill
todos.summary: Pattern: after /forge:approve commit-gate merges a worktree branch, the worktree dir + branch remain because git worktree remove refuses on dirty files (runtime artifacts in worktree's .pipeline/) and bash-guard blocks --force.

todos.text: BUG: worker spawn race recurs when forge_create_run is called twice within ~5s. Observed in this session 2026-05-06 — r-d5ce734d (spawned 5s after r-bd95648b) and r-7f8572d2 (spawned 2s after r-7c0775f4) both produced no pid file and no log file, while their siblings spawned successfully. Both broken runs sit at status=running, stages.plan.status=running, but the worker process never wrote anything. CHANGELOG entry "Worker spawn race condition" claims the per-run worker-task-<runId>.json fix prevents collision when multiple workers spawn concurrently — that fix may be necessary but is insufficient. Investigation surface: mcp/server.js spawn path in forge_create_run + the recursive-spawn guard using findWorkerTaskFile. Hypothesis: race window is in spawn argv preparation or shared file lock rather than the task file itself. Repro: call forge_create_run twice within 5 seconds — second call creates a registry entry but no worker process actually starts.
todos.title: worker spawn race recurs when
todos.summary: Observed in this session 2026-05-06 — r-d5ce734d (spawned 5s after r-bd95648b) and r-7f8572d2 (spawned 2s after r-7c0775f4) both produced no pid file and no log file, while their siblings spawned successfully.

todos.text: BUG: stages.<stage>.agents wholesale-replace bug NOT actually fixed by commit a4d0bb7 (which fixed run.agents). Observed 2026-05-06 in r-e31a1621 (agents trail dropped from 8 to 2 entries), r-0e6834e6 (same pattern), and now r-d5ce734d (stages.plan.agents wiped from ["planner"] at create-time to []). Commit a4d0bb7 added merge-by-agentId to the run-level agents array, but stages.<stage>.agents is a separate field that uses different merge logic (stages map merges by stage key, but the agents subfield within each stage entry is whatever the caller passes). Some writer is passing stages with empty agents and overwriting the original list. Investigation surface: forge_update_run handler stages merge logic in mcp/server.js (around the existing stages merge code), and any caller that passes stages without agents — the merge needs to preserve agents subfield when the caller omits it OR when it's empty.

todos.text: Per-run reviewer-output directory: move from shared docs/context/reviewer-output/ to .pipeline/runs/<runId>/reviewer-output/ so concurrent plan workers don't stomp on each other's reviewer files. Pattern observed 2026-05-06: 5 concurrent plan workers writing to the same docs/context/reviewer-output/ directory means the file mtimes only reflect the LAST worker to write — the conductor cannot reliably inspect a specific run's reviewer outputs. Cited example: r-bd95648b's gate1 hit at 11:14:58 but reviewer-output files showed mtime 11:28:43 (13 min later — belonging to a different run). Surface: scripts/reviewer-dispatch.mjs (write path), agents that read reviewer outputs (planner, implement skill), forge_set_gate or worker code that touches the dir. AC1: reviewer-dispatch.mjs takes runId and writes to .pipeline/runs/<runId>/reviewer-output/<reviewer>.md. AC2: agents reading reviewer outputs receive the runId-scoped path via prompt. AC3: shared docs/context/reviewer-output/ is removed (or kept as legacy fallback for non-run-scoped invocations). AC4: cleanup happens automatically when run reaches terminal status (existing per-run dir cleanup logic).
todos.title: Per-run reviewer-output directory
todos.summary: Pattern observed 2026-05-06: 5 concurrent plan workers writing to the same docs/context/reviewer-output/ directory means the file mtimes only reflect the LAST worker to write — the conductor cannot reliably inspect a specific run's reviewer outputs.

todos.text: Investigate run.agents wholesale-replace regression — commit a4d0bb7 added merge-by-agentId but agents trail still gets wiped late in pipeline. Observed 2026-05-06 in r-bd95648b: agents trail returned [] from forge_get_run after the run hit gate1 (planner + reviewers must have run for the gate to fire, but no records persist). Same pattern in r-d5ce734d (agents=[] after spawn) and r-7f8572d2 (agents had 8 corrupted entries before being marked failed). Distinct from TODO c0892830 which covers stages.<stage>.agents — this one is the top-level run.agents array. Investigation surface: forge_update_run handler at mcp/server.js around the merge-by-agentId logic; ALL writers that touch run.agents (search for 'agents:' in forge_update_run callers); the per-run-active.json merge path at hooks/subagent-stop.js writes too. Hypothesis: writeRun (or whichever path forge_update_run delegates to under the hood) reads existing run.json LATE and uses a stale snapshot, allowing the stage-update merge to overwrite the recent agents writes from SubagentStop. Possible fix: serialize all run.json writes through a single async lock per runId.
todos.title: Investigate run.agents
todos.summary: Observed 2026-05-06 in r-bd95648b: agents trail returned [] from forge_get_run after the run hit gate1 (planner + reviewers must have run for the gate to fire, but no records persist).

todos.text: Persist reviewer verdict body in run.agents[] entry — currently run.agents records outcome (BLOCK/REVISE/APPROVED) but NOT the reasoning text. Observed 2026-05-06 in r-89be3148: a reviewer-boundary BLOCK left only the letter in the agents trail; the verdict body lived only in docs/context/reviewer-output/reviewer-boundary.md, which was overwritten by a later concurrent reviewer-boundary dispatch from another run. The conductor could see "BLOCK happened" but not "BLOCK because X" — and per memory feedback_never_override_blocks the conductor cannot safely approve over a BLOCK without reading the reason. Surface: scripts/reviewer-dispatch.mjs (or wherever reviewer outcomes are recorded), forge_update_run handler agents-array shape (mcp/server.js). AC1: when a reviewer agent completes, the SubagentStop hook (or reviewer-dispatch wrapper) reads the reviewer's output file and stores its content as a verdictBody field on the corresponding run.agents[] entry. AC2: the field is capped at 8KB to avoid run.json bloat (truncate with a "...[truncated]" marker beyond that). AC3: even if the source file is later overwritten or deleted, run.json retains the verdict reasoning. AC4: verdict body is plaintext markdown — no rendering or transformation. AC5: complements (does not replace) TODO 5cefed13 (per-run reviewer-output dir) — both fixes are needed for full durability.
todos.title: Persist reviewer verdict body in
todos.summary: Observed 2026-05-06 in r-89be3148: a reviewer-boundary BLOCK left only the letter in the agents trail; the verdict body lived only in docs/context/reviewer-output/reviewer-boundary.md, which was overwritten by a later concurrent reviewer-boundary dispatch from another run.

todos.text: MCP guard inconsistency: forge_set_gate requires hasGateApprovalToken (mcp/server.js:813), but forge_update_run setting gateState.status="approved" does NOT check the token. This means the conductor can approve a gate via update_run even after the token expired, while set_gate fails. Observed 2026-05-06 in r-bd95648b: forge_set_gate rejected with "Gate approval requires explicit user authorization" while forge_update_run on the same call set gateState.status=approved successfully. Result: drift between run.json (says approved) and .pipeline/gate-pending.json (says pending) until the next set_gate retry. Surface: mcp/server.js — set_gate guard at line 813, update_run handler around line 2116 (the existing transition guard does NOT cover gateState changes). AC1: forge_update_run also calls hasGateApprovalToken when the patch sets gateState.status === "approved" AND the prior gateState.status was "pending"; missing token returns the same error message as set_gate. AC2: update_run STILL accepts gateState.status="rejected" or other fields without the token (only "approved" is gated). AC3: the existing run.status transition guard at the same line is unchanged. AC4: integration test demonstrates that update_run cannot approve a gate without the token.
todos.title: MCP guard inconsistency
todos.summary: This means the conductor can approve a gate via update_run even after the token expired, while set_gate fails.

todos.text: BUG: forge_create_worktree MCP call hangs the worker indefinitely — recurring pattern. Observed 2026-05-06 in r-4e9e406e, r-89be3148, r-7c0775f4: implement worker spawns, calls forge_create_worktree as its first tool_use, gets no response back. Worker pid stays alive but log freezes at the tool_use line. ~30 min later still no progress. Workers had to be SIGTERMed manually each time. Impact: every worktree-using implement worker is at risk; cannot complete any work until the call returns. Surface: mcp/server.js forge_create_worktree handler — investigate whether it deadlocks on a file lock, awaits a hook that never returns, or fails silently in a way the SDK doesn't propagate. Possible causes: (a) lock contention with another MCP tool call (forge_get_run, forge_update_run) racing on .pipeline/runs/<runId>/run.json or singleton, (b) git worktree command hangs on some Windows-specific filesystem state, (c) the response gets dropped and the SDK never receives the tool_result. AC1: investigate via worker log timestamps + MCP server log alignment. AC2: add a timeout on the create_worktree handler so a hung call returns an error instead of hanging the worker indefinitely. AC3: regression test that creates 2-3 worktrees in quick succession and verifies all complete within 30s.

todos.text: Lifecycle cleanup leaves run-active.json pointing to terminal apply runs, creating workflow-guard deadlock. Observed 2026-05-06: r-c4d3cd38 (apply run) reached commit gate, was approved + marked completed, lifecycle cleanup deleted gate-pending.json and docs/context/handoff.md. But run-active.json still pointed to r-c4d3cd38 with pipelineType=apply. workflow-guard.js then chains apply-context checks (requires gate-pending.json + handoff.md) and blocks ALL source-file writes since those files are gone. Conductor cannot edit files until run-active.json is cleared. Direct write to run-active.json is also blocked by workflow-guard. Recovery requires manual `del .pipeline/run-active.json` outside Claude Code (or full session restart so ctx-session-start.js cleans it). Surface: scripts/post-apply-lifecycle.mjs (or whatever runs as part of apply lifecycle) — when the apply run reaches terminal status, also clear/null run-active.json (or update singleton to point at no active run). AC1: post-apply-lifecycle.mjs writes a fresh empty run-active.json (or deletes it via the same path that ctx-session-start uses) when the apply run completes. AC2: alternatively, workflow-guard.js detects when the run it's gating is in terminal status (failed/discarded/completed) and bypasses apply-context checks for that case (fail-open with a stderr warning). AC3: regression test reproduces: complete an apply run, observe run-active.json is cleared and source-file writes work without manual intervention.

todos.text:
Observer card needs more detail + worker retry-with-blocker + researcher always available. Three related improvements:

1. **Surface BLOCK signals on the session card** — currently the observer card shows pipeline/status/gate but not the latest reviewer verdict. When a reviewer BLOCKs, the conductor only learns of it by reading run.json's agents trail manually. Add a `latestBlock` or `attentionFlags` field on dashboard-state that surfaces: latest BLOCK reviewer + agentId, REVISE count, last 30s of progress. Surface as a red marker on the observer card.

2. **Worker should be allowed to retry/iterate with blocker info** — observed 2026-05-06 that workers DO iterate and re-dispatch reviewers after BLOCK (e.g. r-095796d0 iterated post-BLOCK with safety→REVISE, boundary→APPROVED), but this happens silently. Document the retry semantics in skills/plan/SKILL.md so it's explicit; ensure the retry is bounded (max 2 iterations or similar) so we don't infinite-loop. Surface the iteration count on the observer card too (e.g. "iter 2/3").

3. **Researcher always available to worker session** — currently /forge:plan dispatches researcher only if the planner writes a "Research needed" section. When a reviewer BLOCKs with "verify the actual mechanism" type feedback, the worker has no path to re-engage researcher to investigate. Make researcher available as a tool the worker can re-invoke when reviewer feedback demands deeper investigation. Specifically: add researcher to the worker's agent_set unconditionally, and let the worker decide when to fire it.

Surfaces: scripts/forge-observer.mjs (card rendering), mcp/lib/dashboard-state.js (state extraction), skills/plan/SKILL.md (retry + researcher availability), skills/implement/SKILL.md (same), agents/planner.md (signal needs-researcher in handoff).

Justification: observed twice this session — r-89be3148 had a BLOCK that took ~10 min to discover via manual agents-trail read; r-095796d0 BLOCK was visible only after I read run.json. With observer surfacing, decision time drops from minutes to seconds. With worker-retry+researcher availability, the implement-stage feedback loop closes without conductor babysitting.

todos.text: BUG: approval-token.js negation check false-positives against system-reminder context. Observed 2026-05-06: user types literal "approve" but hook's isNegated() (line 55-58 of hooks/approval-token.js) scans 80 chars BEFORE the keyword for negation words ("don't", "do not", "no", "stop", "cancel", "never", "avoid"). The hook reads payload.prompt which Claude Code populates with the user's typed text PLUS system reminders (FORGE conductor rule, anti-speculation rule, observer focus, etc.). The conductor rule contains phrases like "no worker needed", "Do NOT use the Agent tool", "never approve over a BLOCK". When the user's "approve" appears at the end of a prompt with system reminders preceding it, the 80-char lookback catches "no" or "don't" or "never" and marks the keyword negated. Token never written. Result: forge_set_gate fails with "approval requires explicit user authorization" even though user typed the literal word. Recovery has been forge_update_run (which doesn't check token) succeeding but gate-pending.json file drifts. Surface: hooks/approval-token.js — extractUserMessage() at line 51, isNegated() at line 55. Fix candidates: (a) extractUserMessage should strip system-reminder content and return only the user's typed message — Claude Code likely separates these in payload but extractUserMessage falls through to payload.prompt which includes everything; (b) isNegated should not scan across system-reminder boundaries; (c) shorten the 80-char lookback to ~10 chars or scan only within the same line. AC1: user typing literal "approve" with FORGE conductor rules in system context successfully writes the token. AC2: actual negations like "don't approve" or "no approve" still suppress. AC3: regression test with realistic prompt payload (user message + system-reminder boilerplate).

todos.text:
ARCHITECTURE: commit-gate Step 4 protocol is wrong — conductor stages every uncommitted file in the worktree, which includes BLOCKED-phase work. Worker should own ALL commits (per-phase + apply-phase); conductor should ONLY merge.

PROBLEM (concretely observed in r-ea6f9284 2026-05-06):
- Phase 1 reviewer-approved → worker committed (fc1c12d) — correct.
- Phase 2 reviewer-BLOCKED → worker correctly DID NOT commit; changes left uncommitted in worktree (skills/debug/SKILL.md, skills/implement/SKILL.md, skills/plan/SKILL.md, skills/refactor/SKILL.md per `git diff --name-only HEAD` this turn).
- Phase 3 skipped (didn't run).
- Apply phase ran documenter; documenter changes ended up uncommitted in worktree too.
- skills/approve/SKILL.md Step 4.2 instructs the conductor to run `git -C <worktreePath> diff --name-only HEAD` and STAGE EACH FILE INDIVIDUALLY before committing — this would stage the BLOCKED Phase 2 work AND ship it to main.

ROOT CAUSE: Step 4.2 conflates "commit pending apply work" with "commit anything left over". The "anything left over" set includes BLOCKED phases.

FIX (proposal):
1. Worker MUST commit apply-phase work (documenter, lifecycle) inside the worktree before writing the commit gate. Use a worker-side commit instead of expecting the conductor to do it.
2. Worker MUST NOT commit BLOCKED-phase work — leave uncommitted as a marker. Per-phase BLOCK already does this correctly today.
3. skills/approve/SKILL.md Step 4 simplifies to: (a) run `node bin/forge-worktree.js merge <runId>` (or `git merge forge/<runId>`), (b) update run status, (c) handle auto-PR. NO `git -C <wt> diff --name-only HEAD` + stage-each-file step.
4. Step 4.2 / 4.3 (the conductor commit logic) is removed entirely — the worker is the only committer.

AC1: After r-ea6f9284-style scenario (phase 1 committed, phase 2 BLOCKED + uncommitted, phase 3 skipped), running /forge:approve at commit gate merges ONLY fc1c12d into main; the uncommitted phase 2 files stay in the worktree (and are discarded with the worktree on cleanup). AC2: Worker commits documenter/apply-phase work as a `forge: documenter — <feature>` worktree commit before writing commit gate. AC3: skills/approve/SKILL.md Step 4 has no staging or commit instructions for the conductor; only merge + status updates remain. AC4: CLAUDE.md inline commit-gate path (line 21-27) updated to reflect the same simpler flow.

CONTEXT: Discovered by user 2026-05-06 mid-r-ea6f9284 commit gate. Worktree had committed Phase 1 + uncommitted BLOCKED Phase 2 changes. Manual fix this run: skipped /forge:approve flow, did manual `git merge forge/r-ea6f9284` which brings only committed work.

todos.text:
ARCHITECTURE: implement worker should iterate on phase BLOCK or fail the run, not partial-ship. Currently the phased-execution loop in skills/implement/SKILL.md exits on first BLOCK and writes gate2 with phases[] showing partial completion — this leaves the conductor responsible for noticing the partial state and deciding whether to ship.

PROBLEM (concretely from r-ea6f9284 2026-05-06): Phase 1 reviewer-approved + committed; Phase 2 reviewer-BLOCKed + uncommitted; Phase 3 skipped (didn't run). Worker wrote gate2 with phases[1].reviewerVerdict='blocked'. Conductor (me) framed Phase 1 as "foundation, harmless" and merged it — but Phase 1 alone has no callers (no skill injects the prefix yet) so it's effectively dead code.

USER'S ARCHITECTURAL OBSERVATION (2026-05-06): "Is the structure not to loop the implement step until all the phases are done?" — implies BLOCK should drive iteration, not partial ship.

TWO VALID DESIGN CHOICES (today's behaviour is neither):
1. **ITERATE-ON-BLOCK**: when a phase BLOCKs, re-dispatch coder with the BLOCK reviewer feedback, re-run reviewers, repeat until APPROVED or max-iterations (e.g. 2). REVISE/BLOCK is supposed to feed back into the coder loop — this is what the reviewers expect when they emit BLOCK with concrete fix instructions.
2. **FAIL-RUN-ON-BLOCK**: BLOCK = run fails entirely. forge_update_run status=failed with failureReason citing the BLOCKed phase. No gate2, no partial merge. Operator must redo the run.

CURRENT BEHAVIOUR: option 3 (worst) — partial ship with conductor manually filtering. Defaults to merging dead code unless conductor catches it.

PROPOSAL: pick option (1). Implement skill phase loop:
- On phase APPROVED → commit phase, continue to next phase.
- On phase REVISE → re-dispatch coder with REVISE feedback, re-run reviewers (max 2 iterations), then evaluate: APPROVED → commit; still REVISE → mark phase REVISE-unresolved, continue (REVISE is non-blocking by spec); BLOCK → fall to next branch.
- On phase BLOCK → re-dispatch coder with BLOCK feedback, re-run reviewers (max 1 iteration). If APPROVED → commit, continue. If still BLOCK → mark phase blocked, ABORT loop, mark run failed (don't write gate2).

AC1: implement skill SKILL.md phase loop in skills/implement/SKILL.md gains explicit BLOCK-iteration semantics with max-iteration cap. AC2: if final state has any phase still BLOCKed, run goes to status=failed (not gate2-pending). AC3: phases[] still records the per-phase state for audit. AC4: existing per-phase commit semantics preserved (only APPROVED phases commit). AC5: regression test simulates: 3-phase plan, phase 2 BLOCKs first attempt, BLOCK feedback fed back to coder, re-review APPROVED, run completes normally. AC6: regression test simulates: 3-phase plan, phase 2 BLOCKs persistently — run fails, no partial merge, no gate2.
